quizQuestion:
Hoy {___} con el director.
(Today I have spoken with the director.)
sentenceTranslation: Today I have spoken with the director.
explanation: he hablado — present perfect of hablar. Formed with haber (present) + past participle. 'He' = I have (auxiliary). Used for recent or relevant past actions.

quizQuestion:
¿{___} alguna vez con una persona famosa?
(Have you ever spoken with a famous person?)
sentenceTranslation: Have you ever spoken with a famous person?
explanation: has hablado — tú form of present perfect. 'Has' = you have (auxiliary). 'Alguna vez' = ever.

quizQuestion:
Hoy ya {___} dos veces.
(Today I have already eaten twice.)
sentenceTranslation: Today I have already eaten twice.
explanation: he comido — present perfect of comer. Regular -er past participle: com- + -ido.

quizQuestion:
Esta semana {___} mucho ejercicio.
(This week I have done a lot of exercise.)
sentenceTranslation: This week I have done a lot of exercise.
grammarPointLabel: Present perfect: hacer (yo) — he + hecho (irregular PP)
explanation: he hecho — present perfect of hacer. Irregular past participle: hecho (NOT 'hacido'). One of the most common irregular past participles.

quizQuestion:
¿{___} alguna vez esa película?
(Have you ever seen that film?)
sentenceTranslation: Have you ever seen that film?
grammarPointLabel: Present perfect: ver — he visto (irregular PP)
explanation: he visto — present perfect of ver. Irregular past participle: visto (NOT 'vido'). Very common in conversation.

quizQuestion:
¿Qué has {___} hoy?
(What have you done today?)
explanation: hecho — irregular past participle of hacer. Common irregulars to memorize: hacer→hecho, decir→dicho, ver→visto, volver→vuelto, escribir→escrito, poner→puesto.

quizQuestion:
Ya lo he {___} muchas veces.
(I have already said it many times.)
sentenceTranslation: I have already said it many times.
explanation: dicho — irregular past participle of decir (to say/tell). The -cho ending is a pattern for hacer and decir.

quizQuestion:
¿Ha {___} ya de su viaje?
(Has he/she returned from his/her trip already?)
sentenceTranslation: Has he/she returned from his/her trip already?
explanation: vuelto — irregular past participle of volver (to return). Pattern: vowel change in root: vuelto.

quizQuestion:
He {___} tres correos esta mañana.
(I have written three emails this morning.)
sentenceTranslation: I have written three emails this morning.
explanation: escrito — irregular past participle of escribir (to write). The -to ending appears in: escrito, puesto, visto, vuelto.

quizQuestion:
Estoy {___} con mi jefe ahora.
(I am talking with my boss right now.)
sentenceTranslation: I am talking with my boss right now.
explanation: hablando — gerund of hablar. Regular -ar gerund: habl- + -ando. Used with estar to form the present progressive.

quizQuestion:
Estoy {___} en este momento, ¿puedo llamarte luego?
(I am eating right now, can I call you later?)
sentenceTranslation: I am eating right now, can I call you later?
explanation: comiendo — gerund of comer. Regular -er gerund: com- + -iendo.

quizQuestion:
El bebé está {___}.
(The baby is sleeping.)
grammarPointLabel: Present progressive: dormir → durmiendo (o→u stem change)
explanation: durmiendo — gerund of dormir. Stem-change gerund: o→u in the stem: durm- + -iendo. Occurs in -ir verbs with o→ue stem change.

quizQuestion:
Están {___} demasiado dinero.
(They are asking for too much money.)
sentenceTranslation: They are asking for too much money.
grammarPointLabel: Present progressive: pedir → pidiendo (e→i stem change)
explanation: pidiendo — gerund of pedir. Stem-change gerund: e→i: pid- + -iendo. Applies to -ir verbs with e→i stem change.

quizQuestion:
Está {___} en la cama.
(He is reading in bed.)
explanation: leyendo — gerund of leer. When the stem ends in a vowel, the -iendo ending changes to -yendo: le- + yendo.
